TypeSignature.Value: public interface IOrderedQueryable<out T> : System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<out T>, System.Linq.IOrderedQueryable, System.Linq.IQueryable<out T>

.class public interface auto ansi abstract beforefieldinit IOrderedQueryable`1<+ T> implements class System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable`1<!T>, class System.Collections.IEnumerable, class System.Linq.IOrderedQueryable, class System.Linq.IQueryable, class System.Linq.IQueryable`1<!T>
Public Interface IOrderedQueryable(Of Out T) Implements IEnumerable(Of Out T), IOrderedQueryable, IQueryable(Of Out T)
type IOrderedQueryable<'T> = interface interface seq<'T> interface IEnumerable interface IOrderedQueryable interface IQueryable interface IQueryable<'T>
generic <typename T> public interface class IOrderedQueryable : System::Collections::Generic::IEnumerable<T>, System::Linq::IOrderedQueryable, System::Linq::IQueryable<T>

Docs.typeparam: The type of the content of the data source.
Docs.summary: Represents the result of a sorting operation.

remarks.format:
## Remarks The <xref:System.Linq.IOrderedQueryable`1> interface is intended for implementation by query providers.
This interface represents the result of a sorting query that calls the method(s) <xref:System.Linq.Queryable.OrderBy*>, <xref:System.Linq.Queryable.OrderByDescending*>, <xref:System.Linq.Queryable.ThenBy*> or <xref:System.Linq.Queryable.ThenByDescending*>. When <xref:System.Linq.IQueryProvider.CreateQuery``1(System.Linq.Expressions.Expression)> is called and passed an expression tree that represents a sorting query, the resulting <xref:System.Linq.IQueryable`1> object must be of a type that implements <xref:System.Linq.IOrderedQueryable`1>.
For more information about how to create your own LINQ provider, see [LINQ: Building an IQueryable Provider](https://learn.microsoft.com/archive/blogs/mattwar/linq-building-an-iqueryable-provider-part-i).
